metadata.title: DVC + SkyPilot + Factorial Monitor — Double-Check QA Audit
metadata.cost_at_risk: $5-13 (32 GPU jobs x L4 spot @ ~$0.16-0.40 each)

source_plans.plan: SkyPilot Local Test Suite -- Mock SSH and YAML Validation Plan
DVC + SkyPilot Setup Script Test Suite -- Production-Grade Pre-Launch Validation

plan_audit.title: SkyPilot YAML Test Suite

item.planned: Tier 1: Task.from_yaml() structural validation

planned_vs_implemented.item: test_factorial_yaml_parses() in TestSkyPilotYamlParsing uses sky.Task.from_yaml(). Falls back gracefully with pytest.skip() if skypilot not installed.

item.planned: T4 ban in accelerators

planned_vs_implemented.item: TestNoT4.test_no_t4_in_accelerators() checks both dict and string formats.

item.planned: Spot enabled assertion

planned_vs_implemented.item: TestSpotEnabled.test_spot_enabled() verifies use_spot is True.

item.planned: Required env vars declared

planned_vs_implemented.item: TestRequiredEnvVars.test_required_envs_declared() checks 7 required vars. TestRequiredEnvVars.test_hf_token_declared() checks HF_TOKEN separately.

item.planned: No apt-get in setup

planned_vs_implemented.item: TestSetupBannedCommands covers apt-get, uv sync, pip install, git clone.

item.planned: Docker image_id set and uses docker: prefix

planned_vs_implemented.item: TestDockerImage checks image_id exists, starts with docker:, and points to GAR.

item.planned: No bare dvc pull

planned_vs_implemented.item: TestSetupDvcPull.test_no_bare_dvc_pull() verifies path argument exists.

item.planned: Tier 2: sky local up + KinD weekly validation

planned_vs_implemented.item: Plan mentioned as a weekly manual check. Not implemented and not critical for the immediate launch. Acceptable deferral.

item.planned: Test ALL SkyPilot YAMLs in deployment/skypilot/

planned_vs_implemented.item: Only train_factorial.yaml is tested. Plan said to validate ALL YAMLs. Currently there is only one YAML, but the pattern is not parametrized for future YAMLs.

gap.title: No test for file_mounts referencing valid GCS paths

gaps.gap:
train_factorial.yaml uses file_mounts with gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints. No test validates that the file_mounts section exists, references a valid GCS bucket pattern, or uses MOUNT_CACHED mode. If someone changes the bucket name or removes file_mounts, no test catches it.
train_factorial.yaml lines 49-52 define file_mounts with MOUNT_CACHED. test_skypilot_yamls.py has no TestFileMounts class.

gap.title: No test for job_recovery field absence

gaps.gap:
The plan explicitly notes that job_recovery field caused a FAILED_SETUP in SkyPilot v1.0 (it was removed). No test asserts that job_recovery is NOT present in the YAML. If someone adds it back (copying from old docs), the test suite will not catch it.
train_factorial.yaml line 43 has comment: "job_recovery field was removed in SkyPilot v1.0." No test for: assert "job_recovery" not in config

gap.title: No test for setup script checking existing data before pull

gaps.gap:
The actual setup script has an optimization: it checks if data already exists before pulling (if [ -d "data/raw/minivess/imagesTr" ]). This is a correctness feature for spot recovery. No test validates this guard exists.
train_factorial.yaml setup section lines 113-123. No test asserts this skip-if-present logic exists.

gap.title: No test for MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var reference

gaps.gap:
MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI is set to ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} (a shell variable reference). No test validates this critical env var is declared. The required_envs set in the test does not include MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI. If it gets removed from the YAML, all jobs will have empty tracking URI and MLflow logging will fail silently.
train_factorial.yaml line 72: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI: ${MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI} test_skypilot_yamls.py line 130-138: required set does not include MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI.

gap.title: Tests not parametrized over all SkyPilot YAMLs

gaps.gap: Plan says "Add sky.Task.from_yaml() based validation for ALL SkyPilot YAMLs in deployment/skypilot/". Currently only FACTORIAL_YAML is tested. Should use pytest.mark.parametrize with glob("deployment/skypilot/*.yaml") for future-proofing.

gap.title: No test for MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST and PREFECT_DISABLED escape hatches

gaps.gap:
The YAML sets MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 and PREFECT_DISABLED=1 as escape hatches (approved for cloud VMs). No test validates these are present. If removed, the train_flow.py Docker gate and Prefect connection will fail.
train_factorial.yaml lines 78-79. These are critical for the flow to run outside Docker Compose.

gap.title: No test for disk_size >= 100 GB

gaps.gap:
The YAML sets disk_size: 100. DVC data is ~2.7 GB, Docker image is ~8-12 GB, checkpoints can be ~5 GB per model. If disk_size is accidentally reduced, DISK_FULL errors will occur mid-training (wasting the full job cost). No test validates disk_size is sufficient.
train_factorial.yaml line 37: disk_size: 100 ralph_monitor.py has DISK_FULL failure pattern at line 67.

gap.title: No test for setup dvc init --no-scm

gaps.gap:
Setup script runs dvc init --no-scm -f (required because Docker container has no git repo). No test validates this command is present. Without it, DVC commands fail with "not a git repository" error (DVC_NO_GIT pattern in ralph_monitor.py).
train_factorial.yaml line 110: dvc init --no-scm -f ralph_monitor.py line 41-45: DVC_NO_GIT failure pattern.

gap.title: No test for nvidia-smi or PyTorch CUDA check in setup

gaps.gap:
Setup script runs nvidia-smi and a PyTorch CUDA check at the end. These are valuable preflight signals. No test validates their presence.
train_factorial.yaml lines 160-161.

undocumented_features.feature: Validates that the run section checks for splits.json existence. Not explicitly in the plan but matches the DVC plan's T6 (run section guards). Good addition.
Checks that setup has "exit 1" for DVC pull failure. Matches DVC plan T5. Good addition.
Validates GAR europe-north1 region in image_id. Not in the original plan but critical for same-region pull performance. Good addition.

plan_audit.quality_assessment: Core YAML structure validation is solid. The fundamental failure modes from the plan (T4 ban, job_recovery, wrong splits, missing envs) are mostly covered. However, the test suite does not cover file_mounts, disk_size, or several setup script commands that have caused real failures. The biggest gap is the missing MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var test (P1-G4) -- silent MLflow failure is expensive because the job "succeeds" but produces no logged results.

plan_audit.title: SkyPilot Observability / Factorial Monitor Skill

item.planned: Replace table parsing with sky jobs queue -o json

planned_vs_implemented.item: The plan recommends using structured JSON output. ralph_monitor.py still uses text-based parsing of sky jobs queue output (parse_job_status() at line 209). The factorial-monitor SKILL.md references "sky jobs queue" but the actual monitor.md protocol says "Poll sky jobs queue every 60 seconds" without specifying -o json. The existing ralph_monitor.parse_job_status() splits on whitespace and searches for known status strings -- fragile.

item.planned: Batch monitoring (watch ALL 32 jobs in one loop)

planned_vs_implemented.item: The factorial-monitor skill's MONITOR protocol (protocols/monitor.md) defines a polling loop that tracks all jobs. However, this is a SKILL definition (Claude instructions), not running code. There is no Python script that implements the batch monitoring loop.

item.planned: Job-to-condition mapping via JSON manifest

planned_vs_implemented.item: The skill defines factorial_manifest.json (templates/factorial-manifest.json) with proper schema. run_factorial.sh writes job_log.txt (pipe-delimited text), NOT JSON. The manifest JSON is a template, not actually generated by the launcher.

item.planned: Desktop notifications (notify-send)

planned_vs_implemented.item: No notify-send integration anywhere. The plan says "immediate desktop notification on FAILED_SETUP within 30s." Not implemented.

item.planned: Factorial progress matrix (visual grid)

planned_vs_implemented.item: The REPORT protocol (protocols/report.md) shows a factorial grid visualization. This is a skill template, not running code.

item.planned: sky dashboard integration

planned_vs_implemented.item: Plan mentions launching "sky dashboard" alongside the monitor. Not mentioned in the skill or in run_factorial.sh.

item.planned: run_factorial.sh writes JSON mapping file

planned_vs_implemented.item: run_factorial.sh writes pipe-delimited text to JOB_LOG, not a JSON manifest. The format is: "condition_id | model | loss | aux_calib | fold | status" This is NOT parseable as JSON and does not contain job_id values from SkyPilot.

gap.title: run_factorial.sh does not capture SkyPilot job IDs

gaps.gap:
The launcher script does not capture the job_id returned by "sky jobs launch". Without job_ids, the factorial-monitor skill CANNOT map conditions to SkyPilot jobs. The skill's entire Phase 2 (MONITOR) depends on knowing which job_id corresponds to which model x loss x fold condition.
Current behavior: run_factorial.sh writes "LAUNCHED" to the log but does not parse stdout of "sky jobs launch" for the assigned job ID.
The monitor skill would need to use "sky jobs queue" and match by job name (e.g., "dynunet-cbdice_cldice-calibfalse-f0"), which IS available because run_factorial.sh passes --name. This is a workable fallback but fragile.
run_factorial.sh lines 227-239: sky jobs launch output is not captured. Line 241: Only writes "LAUNCHED" status, no job_id.

gap.title: No JSON manifest generation -- only pipe-delimited text log

gaps.gap:
The skill requires factorial_manifest.json (Rule F5). run_factorial.sh generates pipe-delimited text. The gap between the launcher output format and the monitor's expected input format means the MONITOR phase cannot start automatically.
The skill would need to: (1) parse the text log, (2) run sky jobs queue, (3) match by name, (4) build the manifest. This is 4 steps of fragile plumbing that should be a single well-tested function.
run_factorial.sh line 206: "# FORMAT: condition_id | model | loss | aux_calib | fold | status" templates/factorial-manifest.json: expects JSON with job_id, status, etc.

gap.title: ralph_monitor.py still uses text parsing, not -o json

gaps.gap:
parse_job_status() at line 209 of ralph_monitor.py parses whitespace-separated text from sky jobs queue. The plan explicitly says to replace this with "sky jobs queue -o json" for structured, reliable parsing. The current text parsing is fragile -- column positions can change across SkyPilot versions.
ralph_monitor.py lines 209-243: text-based parsing. Plan line 1: "Replace table parsing with sky jobs queue -o json"

gap.title: No cost estimation in monitor or launcher

gaps.gap: The plan mentions cost tracking. The skill's RELAUNCH protocol has cost estimates. But neither run_factorial.sh nor ralph_monitor.py compute or log estimated costs. This means the user has no cost visibility during a run.

gap.title: Factorial monitor skill has no automated tests

gaps.gap: The factorial-monitor skill is defined entirely as markdown protocols and rules. There are no tests verifying that: - The manifest schema is valid JSON - The manifest template matches the expected schema - The eval checklist criteria can be mechanically verified - The skill's phase transitions are logically consistent

gap.title: Kill-switch logic not implementable with current ralph_monitor

gaps.gap: Rule F1 kill-switch: "If 3+ jobs fail with IDENTICAL error within 5 min, cancel same-config jobs." ralph_monitor.py has no batch mode -- it only analyzes one log at a time (analyze_logs() takes a single log string). There is no function to: (1) aggregate failures across multiple jobs, (2) detect same-root-cause count, (3) issue sky jobs cancel commands. The kill-switch is purely aspirational.

undocumented_features.feature: 5-second sleep between job launches prevents SkyPilot API quota issues. Not in the plan but practical. Could cause issues with large factorials (32 jobs x 5s = 160s of just sleeping).
Allows bypassing preflight checks for testing. Not in the plan but practical.

The launcher handles zero-shot baselines (SAM3 vanilla, VesselFM) with special env vars (ZERO_SHOT=true, EVAL_DATASET=deepvess). Not covered by any plan. This is a significant feature that has no dedicated test coverage.

feature.title: POST_TRAINING_METHODS passed as comma-separated env var

undocumented_features.feature: The parent flow iterates over post-training methods internally (none, swag). This design decision (documented in train_factorial.yaml comments) means fewer SkyPilot jobs but more complexity inside each job. Not in the monitor plan.

plan_audit.quality_assessment:
The factorial-monitor skill is well-designed as a SPECIFICATION but poorly realized as IMPLEMENTATION. The skill markdown files define excellent protocols (6 phases, 5 rules, aggregated diagnosis, selective relaunch). However:
1. The launcher (run_factorial.sh) does not produce the manifest the skill expects. 2. ralph_monitor.py is single-job only, not batch-capable. 3. No JSON output from sky jobs queue is used anywhere. 4. No automated tests for any monitoring behavior. 5. The kill-switch is purely aspirational.
For the immediate 4th pass launch, this means: monitoring will be MANUAL (sky jobs queue + sky jobs logs). The skill provides good HUMAN instructions for Claude to follow, but no tooling automation.

plan_audit.title: DVC + SkyPilot Setup Script Test Suite

item.planned: T1: All unfrozen DVC stages have lock entries

planned_vs_implemented.item: TestDvcYamlLockConsistency.test_all_unfrozen_stages_have_lock_entries() in test_dvc_remote_sync.py. Correctly skips frozen stages. Matches plan exactly.

item.planned: T2: SkyPilot dvc pull paths match dvc.lock outputs

planned_vs_implemented.item: TestSkypilotDvcPullPaths.test_dvc_pull_paths_have_lock_entries() and test_no_bare_dvc_pull_in_setup(). Both implemented correctly.

item.planned: T3: Implement DVC sync verification tests

planned_vs_implemented.item: Both T1 and T2 are implemented with yaml.safe_load() as specified.

item.planned: T4: Setup script has no banned commands

planned_vs_implemented.item: Implemented in test_skypilot_yamls.py (TestSetupBannedCommands), not in tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_skypilot_setup_validation.py as planned. The planned file does NOT exist. Functionally equivalent.

item.planned: T5: Setup dvc pull has error handling

planned_vs_implemented.item: Implemented as TestSetupDvcPull.test_setup_has_error_handling_for_dvc() in test_skypilot_yamls.py. The check is simplistic (just checks "exit 1" exists) but catches the basic case.

item.planned: T6: Run section guards against missing prerequisites

planned_vs_implemented.item: TestRunSectionGuards.test_run_checks_splits_file() in test_skypilot_yamls.py.

item.planned: T7: Implement setup script validation tests

planned_vs_implemented.item: Plan had this as a separate GREEN phase for T4-T6. Since they were implemented inline in test_skypilot_yamls.py, T7 is absorbed.

item.planned: T8: Test preflight checks Docker image exists

planned_vs_implemented.item: Plan specified tests/v2/unit/test_cloud_preflight.py. This file does NOT exist. The preflight logic IS in scripts/preflight_gcp.py, but there are no unit tests for the preflight functions themselves.

item.planned: T9: Test preflight checks required env vars

planned_vs_implemented.item: Same as T8 -- test_cloud_preflight.py does not exist. preflight_gcp.py has check_env_vars() but no unit test exercises it.

item.planned: T10: Implement preflight_gcp.py

planned_vs_implemented.item: scripts/preflight_gcp.py exists with 9 checks: Docker image, GCS bucket, checkpoint bucket, DVC data hash, DVC consistency, setup DVC paths, env vars, SkyPilot GCP backend, SkyPilot YAML validity. Exceeds plan scope (plan had 7).

item.planned: T11: Wire run_factorial.sh to call preflight

planned_vs_implemented.item: run_factorial.sh lines 100-113 call preflight_gcp.py before launch. SKIP_PREFLIGHT=1 escape hatch exists as planned.

item.planned: T12: Cloud test -- DVC raw data hash on GCS

planned_vs_implemented.item: Plan specified tests/v2/cloud/test_dvc_gcs_availability.py. This file does NOT exist. The check IS in preflight_gcp.py (check_dvc_data_on_gcs), but there is no pytest-based cloud test with auto-skip.

item.planned: T13: Implement GCS data availability tests

planned_vs_implemented.item: Same as T12.

item.planned: T14: Verify make test-staging catches original failure

planned_vs_implemented.item: Plan says to temporarily revert to broken dvc pull and verify tests catch it. This regression test was not documented as having been run.

item.planned: T15: Verify preflight_gcp.py runs locally

planned_vs_implemented.item: Not documented as verified.

item.planned: T16: Verify run_factorial.sh --dry-run includes preflight

planned_vs_implemented.item: run_factorial.sh skips preflight on dry-run (line 100: if DRY_RUN=false). This CONTRADICTS the plan which says dry-run should show preflight passing. The dry-run path bypasses preflight entirely.

gap.title: No unit tests for preflight_gcp.py functions

gaps.gap:
tests/v2/unit/test_cloud_preflight.py does not exist (planned as T8/T9). preflight_gcp.py has 9 check functions, none tested. If any function has a bug (e.g., wrong GCS hash path construction), it will not be caught until launch time.
The most critical untested function: check_dvc_data_on_gcs() constructs the GCS path as "files/md5/{first2chars}/{remaining}". If DVC changes its storage layout, this silently passes (gsutil ls returns 0 for listing a non-existent prefix).
preflight_gcp.py lines 81-109: check_dvc_data_on_gcs() has manual hash path construction. No unit test validates the path format.

gap.title: No cloud integration test for DVC GCS availability

gaps.gap: tests/v2/cloud/test_dvc_gcs_availability.py does not exist (planned as T12/T13). The preflight script covers this at ad-hoc time, but there is no repeatable test that can be run independently.

gap.title: Dry-run bypasses preflight -- contradicts plan

gaps.gap:
run_factorial.sh line 100: preflight only runs when DRY_RUN=false. Plan T16 says: "run_factorial.sh --dry-run must show preflight checks passing." This means a dry-run will NOT catch preflight failures. A user could do --dry-run (looks fine), remove --dry-run, launch, and THEN hit preflight failure after mental commitment.
run_factorial.sh line 100: if [ "${DRY_RUN}" = false ] Plan T16: "Verify: run_factorial.sh --dry-run includes preflight"

gap.title: validate_data stage has no lock entry and is not frozen

gaps.gap: dvc.yaml has 3 stages: download (frozen, has lock), preprocess (frozen, no lock), validate_data (NOT frozen, no lock, no outs but has metrics). The validate_data stage has metrics (data/validation_report.json with cache: false) but no outs. The test only checks stages with "outs", so validate_data is silently skipped. This is technically correct (metrics with cache:false are not stored in DVC remote), but the test should document why validate_data is excluded.

gap.title: preprocess stage frozen comment check may false-positive

gaps.gap:
TestDvcYamlLockConsistency.test_frozen_stages_have_comments() searches for "frozen: true" lines and checks if adjacent lines have "#". The download stage ALSO has "frozen: true" with a comment. The test iterates ALL frozen stages but only searches for the first "frozen: true" occurrence. If there are multiple frozen stages, it may match the wrong one.
dvc.yaml has TWO frozen stages: download (line 8) and preprocess (line 20). test_dvc_remote_sync.py lines 91-99: finds first "frozen: true" match per stage. The loop breaks after first match, which works only because both have comments.

gap.title: DVC nfiles check hardcodes 300, but actual is 350

gaps.gap:
test_download_stage_has_nfiles() asserts nfiles >= 300. dvc.lock shows nfiles: 350. The threshold is reasonable but not documented. If data is updated and files change, the test may need updating. Should reference the expected count from a config source.
test_dvc_remote_sync.py line 116: assert nfiles >= 300 dvc.lock line 15: nfiles: 350

gap.title: preflight_gcp.py check_dvc_data_on_gcs uses md5, but DVC .dir hashes need special handling

gaps.gap:
The download stage output is a directory (data/raw/minivess/). DVC stores directory manifests with .dir suffix: the hash is "e712f8b23f377a4990b91bef0b1aef76.dir". preflight_gcp.py extracts the md5 field and constructs: gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data/files/md5/e7/12f8b23f377a4990b91bef0b1aef76.dir
This is CORRECT for DVC's GCS layout. However, the code does not handle the .dir suffix explicitly -- it splits on first 2 chars and uses the rest as-is. The ".dir" suffix is preserved because it is part of the hash string in dvc.lock. This WORKS but is not tested. If DVC changes hash format, it will silently pass (gsutil ls on a non-existent prefix returns 0 with empty output, but the check tests returncode == 0 which is True even for empty listings).
CRITICAL: gsutil ls returns exit code 1 for non-existent objects, but exit code 0 for valid but empty paths. Need to verify gsutil ls behavior on exact file paths vs prefix listings.
preflight_gcp.py lines 98-102: hash path construction. dvc.lock line 13: md5: e712f8b23f377a4990b91bef0b1aef76.dir

gap.title: DVC remote config default is minio, not gcs

gaps.gap:
.dvc/config shows default remote is "minio" (localhost:9000). The GCS remote is configured but not default. train_factorial.yaml setup uses "dvc pull ... -r gcs" which is correct. But if someone runs bare "dvc pull" (without -r gcs) in a different context, it will try minio. No test validates that the SkyPilot setup always specifies "-r gcs" explicitly.
.dvc/config line 3: remote = minio train_factorial.yaml line 118: dvc pull data/raw/minivess -r gcs test_dvc_remote_sync.py: checks for -r in pull command but does not assert -r gcs specifically.

undocumented_features.feature: Validates that frozen stages explain WHY they are frozen. Not in the plan but good documentation hygiene.
Specific assertions for the download stage. Not in the plan as separate tests but provide concrete data integrity checks.
Asserts setup pulls data/raw/minivess specifically. Not in the plan but catches wrong path in dvc pull command.

Plan only mentioned DVC data bucket. Implementation also checks the checkpoint bucket (gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints). Good addition -- checkpoint writes failing mid-training would waste the entire job.

plan_audit.quality_assessment:
Module 1 (DVC yaml/lock consistency) is well-implemented with good coverage. Module 2 (setup script validation) is implemented but in a different file than planned. Module 3 (preflight script) is implemented but has ZERO unit tests. Modules 4 and 5 (cloud tests and verification) are completely missing.
The biggest risk: preflight_gcp.py is the last line of defense before spending money, and it has no unit tests. If check_dvc_data_on_gcs() has a bug in hash path construction, it could false-positive (say data exists when it does not).

cross_cutting_analysis.title: Would the current test suite have caught the 4th pass failures?

failure.description: dvc pull -r gcs fails on data/processed/minivess (tracked but not pushed)
failure.caught_by: YES -- preprocess stage has outs but no lock and IS frozen. Wait: preprocess IS frozen. So this test would NOT catch it. The test skips frozen stages. But the actual failure was that bare "dvc pull -r gcs" (without path filter) tried to pull data/processed/minivess which is tracked but not on GCS. The FIX was to use path-specific pull. test_no_bare_dvc_pull catches this. VERDICT: YES, caught by test_no_bare_dvc_pull.
YES -- would verify that dvc pull path has a lock entry.
YES -- would detect bare dvc pull without path filter.

failure.description: job_recovery field unsupported in SkyPilot v1.0
failure.caught_by: MAYBE -- depends on whether sky.Task.from_yaml raises on unknown fields. SkyPilot may ignore unknown fields. No dedicated test for this.
NOT CAUGHT -- no test explicitly checks for absence of job_recovery. Gap P1-G2 covers this.

failure.description: sky binary not on PATH (shell scripting bug)
failure.caught_by: NOT CATCHABLE by YAML tests. This is a shell scripting issue. run_factorial.sh now has explicit sky binary detection (lines 36-44). No test for this but the fix is robust (checks both PATH and .venv/bin/sky).

failure.description: Wrong splits file hardcoded in setup
failure.caught_by: PARTIALLY -- checks that splits.json is referenced in the run section. Does NOT verify that the CORRECT splits file is copied in setup. The setup now uses conditional logic (MAX_TRAIN_VOLUMES > 0 -> debug splits).

cross_cutting_analysis.summary:
The current test suite would catch the $5 FAILED_SETUP incident (F1). It would NOT definitively catch F2 (job_recovery field) -- needs a dedicated test. F3 and F4 are shell scripting issues that YAML tests cannot fully cover.
Overall: the test suite prevents the MOST EXPENSIVE failure mode (F1) but has gaps for lower-cost failure modes that still waste developer time.

task.title: Add tests for file_mounts and disk_size in SkyPilot YAML
task.description: Add TestFileMounts class: - test_file_mounts_exist: config has file_mounts section - test_checkpoint_mount_uses_gcs: /app/checkpoints maps to gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints - test_checkpoint_mount_uses_cached_mode: mode is MOUNT_CACHED (async, non-blocking) Add TestDiskSize class: - test_disk_size_sufficient: disk_size >= 100
task.acceptance_criteria: Tests pass on current YAML. Tests fail if file_mounts removed or disk_size reduced.

task.title: Add MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI to required envs test
task.description: Add MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI to the required env vars set in test_required_envs_declared. Also add MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST and PREFECT_DISABLED (from P1-G6). Also add CHECKPOINT_DIR, LOGS_DIR, SPLITS_DIR (train_flow.py requirements).
task.acceptance_criteria: Test fails if any of these env vars are removed from the SkyPilot YAML.

task.title: Make dry-run include preflight checks
task.description:
Change line 100: remove the DRY_RUN=false condition from the preflight gate. Preflight should run in BOTH dry-run and real mode. In dry-run mode, preflight failures should be reported but not block (warn, don't exit). This way users get preflight feedback before committing to launch.
Alternative: add a separate --preflight-only flag that runs preflight and exits.
task.acceptance_criteria: ./scripts/run_factorial.sh --dry-run configs/factorial/debug.yaml shows preflight check results before listing dry-run job commands.

task.title: Add unit tests for preflight_gcp.py check functions
task.description:
Create tests/v2/unit/test_cloud_preflight.py with: - test_check_dvc_consistency_detects_unlocked_unfrozen_stage (mock dvc.yaml/dvc.lock) - test_check_setup_dvc_paths_detects_bare_pull (mock SkyPilot YAML with bare dvc pull) - test_check_setup_dvc_paths_passes_with_path_filter (mock correct YAML) - test_check_env_vars_detects_missing (mock .env without HF_TOKEN) - test_check_dvc_data_hash_path_construction (verify md5 hash -> GCS path format)
Use tmp_path to create mock files. Do NOT require cloud credentials.
task.acceptance_criteria: All tests pass without cloud credentials. Tests verify the hash path construction for both regular md5 and .dir suffixed md5 hashes.

task.title: Validate gsutil ls behavior in check_dvc_data_on_gcs
task.description:
gsutil ls on a non-existent object returns exit code 1. gsutil ls on a prefix returns exit code 0 even if empty. The current check uses "gsutil ls" on a specific path. Verify this works correctly.
If the hash path format produces a prefix (not exact file), the check could false-positive. Add a check for non-empty stdout: if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
Also: add explicit ".dir" suffix handling comment to document the behavior.
task.acceptance_criteria: check_dvc_data_on_gcs returns False when the hash path does not exist on GCS (verified by DC-4 unit test with mocked subprocess).

task.title: Add test for banned YAML fields (job_recovery, etc.)
task.description: Add TestBannedFields class: - test_no_job_recovery: assert "job_recovery" not in config - test_no_spot_recovery: SkyPilot v1.0 uses use_spot, not spot_recovery These fields caused real FAILED_SETUP incidents.
task.acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if job_recovery or spot_recovery keys are added to the YAML.

task.title: Add test for setup script required commands
task.description: Add TestSetupRequiredCommands class: - test_setup_has_dvc_init: "dvc init" must be in setup (containers have no git) - test_setup_has_no_scm_flag: "dvc init --no-scm" specifically - test_setup_has_dvc_remote_add: "dvc remote add" must be in setup - test_setup_has_gcs_remote: "gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data" in setup - test_setup_has_nvidia_smi: nvidia-smi check at end of setup
task.acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if dvc init or dvc remote add are removed from setup.

task.title: Add test for data-exists-skip optimization in setup
task.description: Verify that setup has a conditional check for existing data before dvc pull. This is critical for spot recovery (preempted VM already has data cached). Test: assert "imagesTr" in setup section (checking for existing data directory).
task.acceptance_criteria: Test fails if the data-exists check is removed from setup.

task.title: Capture job IDs in run_factorial.sh and write JSON manifest
task.description:
After each sky jobs launch, capture stdout for the assigned job ID. SkyPilot prints "Managed job ID: 123" or similar. Parse this and write to a JSON manifest file alongside the existing text log.
Manifest format: outputs/{ts}_factorial_manifest.json matching the template in .claude/skills/factorial-monitor/templates/factorial-manifest.json.
This enables the factorial-monitor skill to work as designed.
task.acceptance_criteria: --dry-run generates a manifest with null job_ids and PENDING status. Real launch generates a manifest with actual job_ids.

task.title: Add JSON parsing mode to ralph_monitor.parse_job_status
task.description: Add parse_job_status_json(json_output: str) that parses sky jobs queue -o json output. Keep the old text parser for backward compat but mark as deprecated. The JSON parser is more reliable and version-independent.
task.acceptance_criteria: Unit test: parse_job_status_json(mock_json) correctly extracts job statuses. Old text parser still works for non-JSON output.

task.title: Add test asserting dvc pull uses -r gcs explicitly
task.description: Current test checks for "-r" flag but not "-r gcs" specifically. .dvc/config default remote is minio (localhost). If someone writes "dvc pull data/raw/minivess -r minio" in the SkyPilot setup, the current test would pass but GCP deployment would fail. Add: assert "-r gcs" in the dvc pull line.
task.acceptance_criteria: Test fails if setup uses "-r minio" or bare "-r" without "gcs".

task.title: Add test for factorial manifest template schema validity
task.description: Validate the factorial-manifest.json template: - Is valid JSON - Has required fields: experiment_id, config_file, factors, jobs - Jobs have required fields: condition, model_family, loss_name, fold, status - Status values are in ALLOWED_STATUSES set

task.title: Parametrize YAML tests over all SkyPilot YAMLs
task.description: Use pytest.mark.parametrize with Path("deployment/skypilot").glob("*.yaml") for tests that apply to ALL SkyPilot YAMLs (parsing, Docker image, banned commands). Keep factorial-specific tests (envs, file_mounts) separate.
task.acceptance_criteria: Adding a new YAML to deployment/skypilot/ automatically includes it in tests.

task.title: Add cost estimation to run_factorial.sh output
task.description: After computing TOTAL_GPU_JOBS, estimate cost: cost = TOTAL_GPU_JOBS * estimated_hours * hourly_rate For L4 spot: ~$0.16/hr. Debug run: ~0.5 hr/job. Print: "Estimated cost: $X.XX (L4 spot, ~0.5 hr/job)"
task.acceptance_criteria: Dry-run output shows estimated cost before listing conditions.

task.title: Add batch failure aggregation to ralph_monitor.py
task.description:
Add aggregate_failures(failures: list[FailureInfo]) -> dict[str, list[FailureInfo]] that groups failures by category. This enables the factorial-monitor skill's DIAGNOSE phase (Rule F2: aggregate before fix).
Also add: detect_kill_switch(failures: list[FailureInfo], time_window: int = 300) that returns True if 3+ failures have identical category within time_window seconds.
task.acceptance_criteria: Unit tests for aggregation and kill-switch detection.

task.title: Create cloud integration test for DVC GCS availability
task.description: Create tests/v2/cloud/test_dvc_gcs_availability.py: - test_raw_data_hash_on_gcs: Parse dvc.lock, verify hash exists on GCS - test_gcs_bucket_accessible: Verify gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data listable Auto-skip without GCS credentials (check for GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS or ADC).
task.acceptance_criteria: Tests pass with GCS credentials, auto-skip without.

task.title: Add test for pre-caching model weights in setup
task.description: Verify setup has HuggingFace model pre-caching logic: - test_setup_precaches_sam3_weights: "sam3" case in setup - test_setup_precaches_vesselfm_weights: "vesselfm" case in setup These prevent GPU time being wasted on weight downloads.
task.acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if model pre-caching logic is removed from setup.

launch_readiness.title: Can we launch the 4th pass debug factorial NOW?
launch_readiness.verdict: CONDITIONAL GO -- fix P0 items first
launch_readiness.rationale:
The most expensive failure mode ($5 FAILED_SETUP from bare dvc pull) IS caught by the current test suite. The SkyPilot YAML is correctly configured with: - Path-specific dvc pull (not bare) - Docker image_id pointing to GAR - L4/A100 accelerators (no T4) - Spot enabled - Error handling on dvc pull failure - splits.json guard in run section - file_mounts for checkpoint persistence
However, 5 P0 gaps create non-trivial risk of another wasted run: 1. DC-1: No test for file_mounts/disk_size (if someone edits YAML before launch) 2. DC-2: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI not in required envs (silent MLflow failure) 3. DC-3: Dry-run skips preflight (user gets false confidence) 4. DC-4: Preflight functions have no unit tests (false positive risk) 5. DC-5: gsutil ls false positive risk in hash check
Recommendation: Fix DC-2 (2 min), DC-3 (5 min), and DC-5 (5 min) before launch. DC-1 and DC-4 can be done after launch if time-pressured.

minimum_pre_launch_tasks.task: Add MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI to required envs (2 min)
Fix gsutil ls false positive (5 min)

post_launch_tasks.task: Capture job IDs and write JSON manifest
Assert -r gcs explicitly in dvc pull test

summary.total_planned_items: 16 (T1-T16 from DVC plan) + 8 (Tier 1 from SkyPilot plan) + 6 (from observability plan)
summary.items_not_implemented: 7 (T8, T9, T12, T13, T14, T15, T16 + JSON monitor + sky dashboard)
summary.total_gaps_found: 18 (9 from P1 + 6 from P2 + 8 from P3, with some overlap)

quality_scores.plan: Good core coverage. Missing file_mounts, disk_size, and some env vars.
Excellent specification, poor implementation. Skill is human-instruction-only. No tooling automation, no JSON manifest, no batch monitoring code.
Modules 1-2 well implemented. Module 3 (preflight) implemented but untested. Modules 4-5 (cloud tests, verification) completely missing.
